statements.1:
Optimization Game

Maximize your score by writing firewall rules that block attacks while allowing legitimate traffic through.
The Goal
You are a network security engineer. Your corporate network is under attack. You are given a list of network traffic flows, each labeled as ATTACK or LEGIT. Your task is to write a limited number of firewall rules that block as many attacks as possible while minimizing collateral damage to legitimate traffic.
Rules

Each firewall rule has the format: ACTION SRC_IP DST_IP PORT PROTOCOL

  • ACTION: ALLOW or BLOCK
  • SRC_IP: exact IP, CIDR notation (e.g. 10.0.0.0/8), or * (wildcard)
  • DST_IP: exact IP, CIDR notation, or *
  • PORT: exact port number, range (e.g. 80-443), or *
  • PROTOCOL: TCP, UDP, ICMP, or *

Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins. If no rule matches, the default action applies.

Scoring
  • Each blocked attack: +10 points
  • Each blocked legitimate flow: -50 points (heavy penalty)

Your goal is to maximize your total score.

Input

Line 1: defaultAction — the default firewall action (ALLOW or BLOCK)

Line 2: maxRules — maximum number of rules you can output

Line 3: N — number of traffic flows

Next N lines: srcIp dstIp port protocol label — one traffic flow per line

Output

Line 1: ruleCount — the number of rules you are submitting (0 to maxRules)

Next ruleCount lines: ACTION SRC_IP DST_IP PORT PROTOCOL

Constraints

8 ≤ maxRules ≤ 20

50 ≤ N ≤ 800

Response time ≤ 5 seconds
